label: legend | venture | heart
sentence:
Legend of the Grain

Long ago, in the heart of the dense Germanic woodlands, a young hunter from a village near the Spessart hills ventured out with his bow and arrows.

label: he | look | valley
sentence: Climbing to the crest of a steep hill, he looked down into a shadowy valley where two clear streams met among ancient oaks and beeches.

label: he | spot | grazing
sentence: There, he spotted a great wild bull grazing close to the water’s edge.

label: hunter | resolve | ∅
sentence: The hunter resolved to wait until the beast moved nearer to the thickets or the riverbank, where he might draw his bow with better aim.

label: sun | sink | forest
sentence: The sun sank behind the sprawling forest, and the bull remained firm.

label: hunter | stay | night
sentence: Throughout the long, chilly night, the hunter stayed awake on the hilltop, hungry and restless, feeling both awe at the wild land and frustration that the beast eluded him.

label: he | climb | dawn
sentence: Before dawn, he climbed once more to his vantage point.

label: bull | stand | ∅
sentence: The bull still stood where it had before—but now it faced east.

label: he | wait | creature
sentence: Again, he waited from dawn till dusk, yet the great creature neither fed nor wandered.

label: ∅ | pass | star
sentence: Another night passed beneath the endless stars, and in the faint morning light, the hunter saw the bull facing south, steadfast and motionless.

label: he | wait | ∅
sentence: He waited all day, heart heavy with wonder and confusion, and when the sun dipped low, the bull had not shifted.

label: hunter | come | ∅
sentence: The hunter came to understand that some mysterious power held the bull in place—no earthly hunger or thirst moved it.

label: hunter | climb | hill
sentence: As sunlight broke the next morning, the hunter climbed the hill only to find the bull was gone.

label: ∅ | grow | plant
sentence: In its place grew a strange, unfamiliar plant.

label: he | examine | ground
sentence: He examined the ground keenly and saw faint impressions where the bull had turned—east, south, west—but no signs showing how it had left.

label: ∅ | spring | shoot
sentence: From the center of these marks sprang the tender green shoots of the new plant.

label: hunter | hurry | ∅
sentence: Filled with both fear and reverence, the hunter hurried back to his village and told the elders and chieftains of this marvelous vision, of the silent bull and the plant born from its standing place.

label: villager | go | ∅
sentence: Together, the villagers went to see, and they found the strange crop protected by the very tracks the bull had made, yet no way in or out.

label: they | know | ∅
sentence: They knew then that Wodan had sent this gift to them—a sacred plant unlike any they had known.

label: they | remember | turning
sentence: They remembered the turning of seasons and the time when each forest bounty was ready to be harvested.

label: they | guard | ∅
sentence: Watching carefully, they guarded what had sprung from the earth with solemn care.

label: flower | protrude | growth
sentence: Soon, a delicate flower blossomed upon the plant, and from one knobby joint protruded a curious growth covered in soft hairs.

label: they | brown | autumn
sentence: At first, the hairs were a gentle green, then they browned with the coming autumn.

label: people | whisper | ∅
sentence: The people whispered that the fruit might be ripe, yet none dared touch it.

label: they | gather | ∅
sentence: They gathered, unsure, until a young man spoke boldly: "My path has known hardship and misfortune.

label: fate | seal | ∅
sentence: If this gift harms me, my fate is sealed regardless.

label: he | lay | hand
sentence:
"

Encouraged by his courage, he laid his hand upon the plant and carefully grasped the fruit.

label: he | declare | ∅
sentence: Holding it firm, he declared, "It is firm, it is ripe."

label: he | reveal | kernel
sentence: Gently peeling back the protective husks, he revealed kernels of red gold glistening in the muted light.

label: he | hold | grain
sentence: He held the grains to the crowd and tasted some himself.

label: he | fall | ∅
sentence: He did not fall ill.

label: people | know | moment
sentence: In that moment, the people knew Wodan had sent them this sacred food to nourish their bodies and their souls.

label: stalk | gather | ∅
sentence: When the birch leaves turned tawny with the approach of frost, the stalks too browned and the golden fruits were gathered, stored carefully for the winter months.

label: kernel | divide | household
sentence: After the snow melted and the earth thawed, the kernels were divided among the households, four for each family to plant and cherish.

label: people | move | house
sentence: The people moved their humble houses closer to the blessed hill where the plant had first appeared.

label: they | mound | earth
sentence: When spring returned and the hills were lush once more, they mounded the earth as it had been when the plant first sprouted, planting the sacred seed with reverence.

label: stalk | grow | ∅
sentence: The stalks grew tall and strong, bearing ears blushing with colors of deep red, bright gold, creamy white, and a rare dark blue.

label: harvest | multiply | year
sentence: In the years that followed, the harvest multiplied, and the villagers welcomed neighbors from distant clans to share in this new bounty.

label: gift | spread | they
sentence: Through them, the gift spread across the forests and villages far and wide, bringing the blessing of the grain—the symbol of Wodan’s favor and the promise of life—to all who dwelled in the shadow of the great oaks.
